We found such a relation in our MCI subgroup for all segmentation methods, but not in the cognitively normal or AD groups. These results contrasted with previously reported relationships in AD between the delayed subtest of RAVLT with HOC in a Brazilian sample [@sudo2019] and with HCvol within ADNI [@wangx2019].

Further, HVR associations with memory were comparable to those of HCvol among all segmentations and subgroups. While these results contrast with the preliminary report of HVR surpassing an already high association between HCvol and memory measured by the same test, they fit with the heterogeneous and highly variable existing evidence for the relationship between HCvol and memory performance [@vanpetten2004]. Another potential source of difference between our results and those reported by Schoemaker et al. [@schoemaker2019] is that our groups were on average a decade older, and greater individual variability in episodic memory has been found for older adults [@morse1993; @nelson1992; @verhaeghen1990; @wilson2002]. Additionally, given the clinical nature of our sample, we also explored the association between our hippocampal measures and a more global evaluation of cognition in the form of the ADAS13, which, in addition to memory, assesses other domains like language and praxis [@kueper2018]. Previous studies have found a relationship between the HC and global cognitive functioning measured with the mini-mental status examination (MMSE) in only AD [@vipin2018], or AD and MCI but not normal controls [@peng2015]. Despite these results and the fact that ADAS13 is designed to evaluate cognitive dysfunction in the early stages of AD, we did find a significant association with HCvol in our healthy cohorts. For cognitively normal and MCI subjects, the associations with global cognition of both HVR and HCvol were comparable. Contrary to the abundant evidence of the relationship between the HC and general cognition in AD [@vipin2018; @peng2015; @morrison2023a; @morrison2023b], the correlations in our AD cohort were lower compared to the other subgroups, with only HVRs reaching statistical significance across all segmentation methods. This advantage of HVR over HCvol was statistically significant for the MALF segmentations of the MCI and AD cohorts, further suggesting that HVR might be particularly superior when only lower quality segmentations are available.

Our study is not without limitations. We chose the ADNI cohort for its extensive clinical and imaging data and its standardization in Alzheimer's research, making it an ideal dataset for validating the CNN-based HVR calculation. Nonetheless, this cohort consists mostly of highly educated white individuals, which may limit the generalizability of our findings. While the inclusion of an internal out-of-sample validation enhances the robustness of our approach within ADNI, additional analyses across demographically diverse and externally sourced datasets are still necessary to fully establish generalizability. Additionally, while out-of-group analyses with other diseases characterized by enlarged ventricles such as schizophrenia would provide valuable insights into the specificity of HVR as a biomarker, these require distinct hypotheses, methodologies, and clinical expertise that lie beyond the scope of this study. Future research may explore such comparisons to further evaluate the versatility of HVR across various neurodegenerative and psychiatric conditions.

By using only the baseline MRI of ADNI, this study focused on cross-sectional data as a foundational step to validate the proposed segmentation method in a large and diverse sample. We did not assess the longitudinal utility of HVR, such as its ability to track disease progression or predict conversation from MCI to AD, which future work should address. Longitudinal studies will be crucial for understanding hippocampal atrophy dynamics over time and their association with cognitive decline, which we plan to explore in future research. In addition, we did not look into the effects of sex, even though there were substantial sex differences reported in the original paper on HVR [@schoemaker2019]. This omission was deliberate to avoid increasing the already large number of statistical comparisons in this paper. Nonetheless, we did observe minor laterality effects in segmentation performance, particularly in the MCI and AD groups of the out-of-sample validation dataset, suggesting that laterality may play a more nuanced role that merits further investigation. That being said, these variables are crucial for understanding hippocampal variability; in particular the analysis of the effect of sex or gender in medio-temporal degeneration measured with HVR, which will be the focus of future work.

Another limitation is related to the choice of segmentation methods we evaluated. Given the wide use of the FreeSurfer pipeline, we decided to include it in our selection of evaluated methods even though it was impossible to evaluate its accuracy without its gold standards, and unfair to compare it to our gold standards. Because matching manual segmentations for FreeSurfer were unavailable, we were unable to compute overlap-based performance metrics such as Dice or Kappa for this method, limiting our ability to assess its segmentation accuracy directly. Relatedly, its comparison of calculated HVR values to our trained methods was tainted by different anatomical definitions of the HC and the surrounding ventricles compared to the other methods. Despite these differences, the high correlation to our proposed CNN method together with the comparable CH:AD effect size and associations with age and global cognition support its use as a viable alternative to our proposed CNN method.


# Conclusions {#sec-conclusions}

In conclusion, after evaluating various automatic segmentation methods, with Dice Kappas of 0.96 and rho values of 0.99 against high-quality manually segmented labels, the CNN method yields the best HC segmentations among the three methods, with results that are comparable to the best in the literature. The high HCvol correlations between FreeSurfer, CNN, and NLPB methods show that these methods agree fairly well for the segmentation of the HC, and thus all are viable choices to compute the HVR in large samples. HVR, when compared against HCvol, showed greater consistency across segmentation methods, a larger effect size when distinguishing between individuals with AD and healthy controls, and stronger associations with age and global cognition, particularly improving the performance of the MALF method. These advantages held across clinical groups and generalized well to an out-of-sample validation dataset. These findings support the promise of HVR as a measure of hippocampal integrity, specifically for neurodegenerative disease research and the study of age-related cognitive decline.


# Author Contributions {.unnumbered}

**Sofia Fernandez-Lozano:** conceptualization, methodology, software, investigation, writing – original draft, visualization. **Vladimir Fonov:** software, data curation. **Dorothee Schoemaker:** resources, writing – review and editing. **Jens Pruessner:** resources, writing – review and editing. **Olivier Potvin:** resources, writing – review and editing. **Simon Duchesne:** resources, writing – review and editing. **D. Louis Collins:** conceptualization, writing – review and editing, supervision.


# Acknowledgements {.unnumbered}

Data collection and sharing for this project was funded by the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (National Institutes of Health Grant No. U01 AG024904) and DOD Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (Department of Defense award number W81XWH-12-2-0012). Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative is funded by the National Institute on Aging, the National Institute of Biomedical Imaging and Bioengineering, and through generous contributions from the following: AbbVie, Alzheimer's Association; Alzheimer's Drug Discovery Foundation; Araclon Biotech; BioClinica Inc.; Biogen; Bristol-Myers Squibb Company; CereSpir Inc.; Cogstate; Eisai Inc.; Elan Pharmaceuticals Inc.; Eli Lilly and Company; EuroImmun; F. Hoffmann-La Roche Ltd. and its affiliated company Genentech Inc.; Fujirebio; GE Healthcare; IXICO Ltd.; Janssen Alzheimer Immunotherapy Research & Development LLC.; Johnson & Johnson Pharmaceutical Research & Development LLC.; Lumosity; Lundbeck; Merck & Co. Inc.; Meso Scale Diagnostics LLC.; NeuroRx Research; Neurotrack Technologies; Novartis Pharmaceuticals Corporation; Pfizer Inc.; Piramal Imaging; Servier; Takeda Pharmaceutical Company; and Transition Therapeutics. The Canadian Institutes of Health Research is providing funds to support Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative clinical sites in Canada. Private sector contributions are facilitated by the Foundation for the National Institutes of Health (www.fnih.org). The grantee organization is the Northern California Institute for Research and Education, and the study is coordinated by the Alzheimer's Therapeutic Research Institute at the University of Southern California. Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative data are disseminated by the Laboratory for Neuro Imaging at the University of Southern California.


# Conflicts of Interest {.unnumbered}

The authors declare no conflicts of interest.


# Data Availability Statement {.unnumbered}

The data that support the findings of this study are available from Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative at <https://adni.loni.usc.edu/>.


# References {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::


<!--
================================================================================
SUPPLEMENTARY MATERIALS
================================================================================
-->

{{< pagebreak >}}

# Supplementary Materials {.unnumbered .appendix}

```{=latex}
\setcounter{table}{0}
\renewcommand{\thetable}{S\arabic{table}}
\setcounter{figure}{0}
\renewcommand{\thefigure}{S\arabic{figure}}
```

```{r}
#| label: supp-setup
#| include: false

# Load additional packages for supplementary materials
library(stringr)
library(progress)

# Helper function to load RDS or generate table
load_or_generate <- function(rds_path, generate_fn) {
  if (file.exists(rds_path)) {
    readRDS(rds_path)
  } else {
    tbl <- generate_fn()
    saveRDS(tbl, rds_path)
    tbl
  }
}

# Load segmentation kappa data for supplementary tables/figures
supp_segm_kappa <- list()
for (segm in c("malf", "nlpb", "cnn")) {
  fpath <- here("data/derivatives", sprintf("man-seg_kappa_hcvc_%s.csv", segm))
  if (file.exists(fpath)) {
    supp_segm_kappa[[toupper(segm)]] <- fread(fpath)
  }
}

# Load validation data
supp_valid_kappa <- NULL
fpath <- here("data/derivatives/man-seg_kappa_hc_cnn_adni.csv")
if (file.exists(fpath)) {
  supp_valid_kappa <- fread(fpath)
}

# Load validation groups
supp_valid_groups <- NULL
fpath <- here("data/adni_validation_groups.csv")
if (file.exists(fpath)) {
  supp_valid_groups <- fread(fpath) |>
    setnames(c("ID", "GROUP")) |>
    (\(DT) DT[, GROUP := factor(GROUP, levels = c("NC", "MCI", "AD"),
                                 labels = c("CH", "MCI", "AD"))])()
}

# Prepare training data (from cross-validation segmentations)
Map(
  \(Name, DT) {
    DT[, let(
      segm = factor(Name),
      id = factor(id),
      roi = roi |> toupper() |> factor(),
      side = side |> substr(1, 1) |> toupper() |> factor()
    )]
    DT |> setcolorder("segm") |> setnames(toupper)
  },
  names(supp_segm_kappa),
  supp_segm_kappa
) |> invisible()

supp_training_data <- supp_segm_kappa |>
  rbindlist() |>
  melt(id = 1:4, variable = "MSR", value = "VAL")

# Prepare validation data
supp_validation_data <- NULL
if (!is.null(supp_valid_kappa) && !is.null(supp_valid_groups)) {
  supp_valid_kappa[, let(
    dataset = "ADNI",
    id = factor(id),
    side = hc |> substr(4, 4) |> toupper() |> factor()
  )][, hc := NULL]
  supp_valid_kappa |>
    setcolorder(c("dataset", "id", "side")) |>
    setnames(toupper)
  supp_validation_data <- supp_valid_groups[supp_valid_kappa, on = "ID"] |>
    melt(id = 1:4, variable = "MSR", value = "VAL")
}

# Run statistical analyses
supp_comparisons <- list()

## Cross-validation: Repeated measures robust ANOVA
supp_comparisons$Training <- list(ANOVA = list(), PostHoc = list())

for (roi in levels(supp_training_data$ROI)) {
  for (msr in levels(supp_training_data$MSR)) {
    aov_res <- tryCatch({
      supp_training_data[
        roi, on = "ROI"
      ][
        msr, on = "MSR"
      ][
        order(SEGM, SIDE),
        rmanova(VAL, interaction(SEGM, SIDE), ID)
      ] |> suppressWarnings()
    }, error = \(e) NULL)

    if (!is.null(aov_res)) {
      identifier <- paste(roi, msr, sep = "_")
      supp_comparisons$Training$ANOVA[[identifier]] <- data.table(
        ROI = roi,
        MSR = msr,
        Fstat = aov_res$test,
        DF = sprintf("(%.2f, %.2f)", aov_res$df1, aov_res$df2),
        Pval = aov_res$p.value,
        Padj = p.adjust(aov_res$p.value, method = "fdr", n = 10)
      )

      posthoc <- supp_training_data[
        roi, on = "ROI"
      ][
        msr, on = "MSR"
      ][
        order(SEGM, SIDE),
        rmmcp(VAL, interaction(SEGM, SIDE), ID)
      ] |> suppressWarnings()

      supp_comparisons$Training$PostHoc[[identifier]] <- posthoc$comp |>
        data.table() |>
        setnames(1:2, paste0("G", 1:2)) |>
        (\(DT) DT[, let(
          G1 = factor(G1, labels = posthoc$fnames[-6]),
          G2 = factor(G2, labels = posthoc$fnames[-1]),
          ROI = roi,
          MSR = msr
        )])()
    }
  }
}

## Out-of-sample validation: Robust mixed ANOVA
supp_comparisons$Validation <- list(ANOVA = list(), PostHoc = list())

if (!is.null(supp_validation_data)) {
  for (msr in levels(supp_validation_data$MSR)) {
    aov_res <- tryCatch({
      bwtrim(VAL ~ GROUP * SIDE, ID, supp_validation_data[msr[1], on = "MSR"])
    }, error = \(e) NULL)

    if (!is.null(aov_res)) {
      aov.dt <- data.table(
        ROI = "HC",
        MSR = msr,
        COMP = c("GROUP", "SIDE", "GROUP:SIDE"),
        Fstat = sprintf(
          "%.2f", as.numeric(aov_res[c("Qa", "Qb", "Qab")])
        ),
        DF = sprintf(
          "(%i, %.2f)",
          aov_res[c("A.df", "B.df", "AB.df")] |>
            lapply(\(x) x[[1]]) |> unlist(),
          aov_res[c("A.df", "B.df", "AB.df")] |>
            lapply(\(x) x[[2]]) |> unlist()
        ),
        Pval = as.numeric(aov_res[c("A.p.value", "B.p.value", "AB.p.value")])
      )
      aov.dt[, Padj := p.adjust(Pval, method = "fdr", n = 5)]
      supp_comparisons$Validation$ANOVA[[msr]] <- aov.dt

      if (aov.dt["GROUP", on = "COMP", Pval <= 0.05]) {
        posthoc <- lincon(
          VAL ~ GROUP, supp_validation_data[msr, on = "MSR"]
        )
        supp_comparisons$Validation$PostHoc[[msr]] <- posthoc$comp |>
          data.table() |>
          setnames(1:2, paste0("G", 1:2)) |>
          (\(DT) DT[, let(
            G1 = factor(G1, labels = posthoc$fnames[-3]),
            G2 = factor(G2, labels = posthoc$fnames[-1]),
            MSR = msr
          )])()
      }
    }
  }
}

# Unlist comparison results
supp_comparisons <- lapply(
  supp_comparisons,
  \(level1) lapply(level1, rbindlist)
)

# Compute post-hoc segmentation comparison data for tables S4 & S5
supp_posthoc_segm <- copy(
  supp_comparisons$Training$PostHoc[
    substr(G1, 1, 1) != substr(G2, 1, 1)
  ]
) |>
  (\(DT) {
    DT[, c("G1", "G1s") := DT[, tstrsplit(G1, "\\.")]]
    DT[, c("G2", "G2s") := DT[, tstrsplit(G2, "\\.")]]
    DT[, COMP := sprintf("%s-%s \u2014 %s-%s", G1, G1s, G2, G2s)]
    DT[MSR %like% "ACC" & psihat == 0, COMP := "MALF \u2014 NLPB"]
    DT[, COMP := factor(COMP, levels = DT[order(G2), unique(COMP)])]
    DT[
      ,
      .(
        CONTRAST = COMP,
        PSI = fcase(
          psihat == 0, "0",
          abs(psihat) < 0.01, sprintf("%.1e", psihat),
          abs(psihat) >= 0.01, sprintf("%.2f", psihat)
        ),
        CI = sprintf(
          "(%s, %s)",
          fcase(
            ci.lower == 0, "0",
            abs(ci.lower) < 0.01, sprintf("%.1e", ci.lower),
            abs(ci.lower) >= 0.01, sprintf("%.2f", ci.lower)
          ),
          fcase(
            ci.upper == 0, "0",
            abs(ci.upper) < 0.01, sprintf("%.1e", ci.upper),
            abs(ci.upper) >= 0.01, sprintf("%.2f", ci.upper)
          )
        ),
        Pval = fifelse(is.na(p.value), NA, sprintf("%.3f", p.value)),
        SIGN = p.value < p.crit
      ),
      .(
        ROI,
        MSR = MSR |> str_to_title() |> sprintf(fmt = "**%s**")
      )
    ]
  })() |>
  unique() |>
  dcast(... ~ ROI, value.var = c("PSI", "CI", "Pval", "SIGN")) |>
  setcolorder(c(1:3, 5, 7, 9))
```

## Supplementary Tables {.unnumbered}

### Table S1: Cross-validation segmentation performance {.unnumbered}

```{r}
#| label: tbl-s1
#| tbl-cap: "**Automatic segmentation performance metrics.** Performance metrics of the three cross-validation trained automated segmentation methods (MALF, NLPB, and CNN) for both the hippocampus (HCvol) and the surrounding ventricular regions (CSFvol). Overall-agreement and class-wise performance metrics include accuracy, Dice coefficient, Cohen's Kappa, sensitivity and specificity, with separate values reported for the left and right hemispheres. All values are reported as medians with standard deviations."

rds_path <- here("tables/table-s1_man-seg_hcv-hvr_cv.rds")

tbl_s1 <- load_or_generate(rds_path, function() {
  supp_training_data[
    ,
    .(VAL = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", median(VAL), sd(VAL))),
    .(
      SEGM = factor(SEGM, labels = sprintf("**%s**", c("MALF", "NLPB", "CNN"))),
      MSR = str_to_title(MSR),
      ROI_SIDE = paste(ROI, SIDE, sep = "_")
    )
  ] |>
    dcast(... ~ ROI_SIDE, value.var = "VAL") |>
    gt(rowname_col = "MSR", groupname_col = "SEGM", process_md = TRUE) |>
    tab_stub_indent(rows = everything(), indent = 1) |>
    cols_align("center", columns = c("HC_L", "HC_R", "VC_L", "VC_R")) |>
    tab_spanner(label = "Hippocampus", columns = starts_with("H"), level = 2) |>
    tab_spanner(label = "Ventricles", columns = starts_with("V"), level = 2) |>
    cols_label(ends_with("L") ~ "Left", ends_with("R") ~ "Right") |>
    style_manuscript_table(table_type = "supplementary")
})

tbl_s1
```

\newpage

### Table S2: Cross-validation ANOVA results {.unnumbered}

```{r}
#| label: tbl-s2
#| tbl-cap: "**Two-way ANOVA results for segmentation metrics.** Results of the robust repeated-measures two-way ANOVA with trimmed means applied to the hippocampal and ventricular segmentation performance measures comparing the automated segmentation methods and hemispheres. Reported are the F-statistics, degrees of freedom, and *p*-values. *P*-values have been adjusted for FDR using the Benjamini and Hochberg procedure."

rds_path <- here("tables/table-s2_man-seg_anova_cv.rds")

tbl_s2 <- load_or_generate(rds_path, function() {
  supp_comparisons$Training$ANOVA[
    order(MSR),
    .(Fstat, DF, Pval = sprintf("%.3f", Padj)),
    .(
      ROI = fifelse(ROI == "HC", "**Hippocampus**", "**Ventricles**"),
      MSR = str_to_title(MSR)
    )
  ] |>
    gt(rowname_col = "MSR", groupname_col = "ROI", process_md = TRUE) |>
    tab_stub_indent(rows = everything(), indent = 1) |>
    cols_align("center", columns = c("Fstat", "DF", "Pval")) |>
    cols_label(
      "Fstat" ~ md("*F*"), "DF" ~ "df", "Pval" ~ md("*p*")
    ) |>
    style_manuscript_table(table_type = "supplementary")
})

tbl_s2
```

\newpage

### Table S3: Post-hoc comparisons (Side differences) {.unnumbered}

```{r}
#| label: tbl-s3
#| tbl-cap: "**Pair-wise comparisons for segmentation metrics by side.** Robust pair-wise post hoc comparisons of side differences in performance measures within the automated method for hippocampal and ventricular segmentations. The comparisons include robust effect sizes ($\\psi$) with their 95% confidence intervals and corresponding *p*-values. Bold entries indicate statistical significance after correction for multiple comparisons."

rds_path <- here("tables/table-s3_man-seg_posthoc_cv_side.rds")

tbl_s3 <- load_or_generate(rds_path, function() {
  supp_comparisons$Training$PostHoc[
    substr(G1, 1, 1) == substr(G2, 1, 1),
    .(
      PSI = fcase(
        psihat == 0, "0",
        abs(psihat) < 0.01, sprintf("%.1e", psihat),
        abs(psihat) >= 0.01, sprintf("%.2f", psihat)
      ),
      CI = sprintf(
        "(%s, %s)",
        fcase(
          ci.lower == 0, "0",
          abs(ci.lower) < 0.01, sprintf("%.1e", ci.lower),
          abs(ci.lower) >= 0.01, sprintf("%.2f", ci.lower)
        ),
        fcase(
          ci.upper == 0, "0",
          abs(ci.upper) < 0.01, sprintf("%.1e", ci.upper),
          abs(ci.upper) >= 0.01, sprintf("%.2f", ci.upper)
        )
      ),
      Pval = fifelse(is.na(p.value), NA, sprintf("%.3f", p.value)),
      SIGN = p.value < p.crit
    ),
    .(
      ROI,
      SEGM = G1 |>
        gsub(pattern = "\\.(L|R)", replacement = "") |>
        factor(
          levels = c("MALF", "NLPB", "CNN"),
          labels = sprintf("**%s**", c("MALF", "NLPB", "CNN"))
        ),
      MSR = str_to_title(MSR)
    )
  ] |>
    dcast(... ~ ROI, value.var = c("PSI", "CI", "Pval", "SIGN")) |>
    setorder(MSR) |>
    setcolorder(c(1:3, 5, 7, 9)) |>
    gt(
      rowname_col = "MSR",
      groupname_col = "SEGM",
      process_md = TRUE
    ) |>
    tab_style(
      style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
      locations = cells_body(
        columns = c("PSI_HC", "CI_HC", "Pval_HC"),
        rows = SIGN_HC == TRUE
      )
    ) |>
    tab_style(
      style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
      locations = cells_body(
        columns = c("PSI_VC", "CI_VC", "Pval_VC"),
        rows = SIGN_VC == TRUE
      )
    ) |>
    cols_hide(columns = contains("SIGN")) |>
    tab_stub_indent(rows = everything(), indent = 1) |>
    cols_align("center", columns = everything()) |>
    opt_horizontal_padding(scale = .5) |>
    tab_spanner(label = "Hippocampus", columns = ends_with("HC")) |>
    tab_spanner(label = "Ventricles", columns = ends_with("VC")) |>
    cols_label(
      starts_with("PSI") ~ md("$\\psi$"),
      starts_with("CI") ~ md("$95\\%~\\text{CI}$"),
      starts_with("Pval") ~ md("$p$")
    ) |>
    style_manuscript_table(table_type = "supplementary")
})

tbl_s3
```

\newpage

### Table S4: Post-hoc comparisons (Accuracy, Dice, Kappa) {.unnumbered}

```{r}
#| label: tbl-s4
#| tbl-cap: "**Pair-wise comparisons for segmentation metrics by overall-agreement performance.** Robust pair-wise post hoc comparisons of overall-agreement performance (accuracy, Dice coefficient and Cohen's kappa) between the different automated methods for hippocampal and ventricular segmentations. The comparisons include robust effect sizes ($\\psi$) with their 95% confidence intervals and corresponding *p*-values. Bold entries indicate statistical significance after correction for multiple comparisons."

rds_path <- here("tables/table-s4_man-seg_posthoc_cv_segm1.rds")

tbl_s4 <- load_or_generate(rds_path, function() {
  supp_posthoc_segm[!MSR %like% "S"] |>
    gt(
      rowname_col = "CONTRAST",
      groupname_col = "MSR",
      process_md = TRUE
    ) |>
    tab_style(style = cell_text(size = "x-small"), locations = cells_body()) |>
    tab_style(style = cell_text(size = "x-small"), locations = cells_stub()) |>
    tab_style(
      style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
      locations = cells_body(
        columns = c("PSI_HC", "CI_HC", "Pval_HC"),
        rows = SIGN_HC == TRUE
      )
    ) |>
    tab_style(
      style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
      locations = cells_body(
        columns = c("PSI_VC", "CI_VC", "Pval_VC"),
        rows = SIGN_VC == TRUE
      )
    ) |>
    cols_hide(columns = contains("SIGN")) |>
    tab_stub_indent(rows = everything(), indent = 1) |>
    cols_align("center", columns = everything()) |>
    opt_horizontal_padding(scale = .3) |>
    tab_spanner(label = "Hippocampus", columns = ends_with("HC")) |>
    tab_spanner(label = "Ventricles", columns = ends_with("VC")) |>
    cols_label(
      starts_with("PSI") ~ md("$\\psi$"),
      starts_with("CI") ~ md("$95\\%~\\text{CI}$"),
      starts_with("Pval") ~ md("$p$")
    ) |>
    tab_options(table.font.size = "8pt", column_labels.font.size = "8pt") |>
    style_manuscript_table(table_type = "supplementary")
})

tbl_s4
```

\newpage

### Table S5: Post-hoc comparisons (Sensitivity, Specificity) {.unnumbered}

```{r}
#| label: tbl-s5
#| tbl-cap: "**Pair-wise comparisons for segmentation metrics by class-wise performance.** Robust pair-wise post hoc comparisons of class-wise performance (sensitivity and specificity) between the different automated methods for hippocampal and ventricular segmentations. The comparisons include robust effect sizes ($\\psi$) with their 95% confidence intervals and corresponding *p*-values. Bold entries indicate statistical significance after correction for multiple comparisons."

rds_path <- here("tables/table-s5_man-seg_posthoc_cv_segm2.rds")

tbl_s5 <- load_or_generate(rds_path, function() {
  supp_posthoc_segm[MSR %like% "S"] |>
    gt(
      rowname_col = "CONTRAST",
      groupname_col = "MSR",
      process_md = TRUE
    ) |>
    tab_style(style = cell_text(size = "x-small"), locations = cells_body()) |>
    tab_style(style = cell_text(size = "x-small"), locations = cells_stub()) |>
    tab_style(
      style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
      locations = cells_body(
        columns = c("PSI_HC", "CI_HC", "Pval_HC"),
        rows = SIGN_HC == TRUE
      )
    ) |>
    tab_style(
      style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
      locations = cells_body(
        columns = c("PSI_VC", "CI_VC", "Pval_VC"),
        rows = SIGN_VC == TRUE
      )
    ) |>
    cols_hide(columns = contains("SIGN")) |>
    tab_stub_indent(rows = everything(), indent = 1) |>
    cols_align("center", columns = everything()) |>
    opt_horizontal_padding(scale = .3) |>
    tab_spanner(label = "Hippocampus", columns = ends_with("HC")) |>
    tab_spanner(label = "Ventricles", columns = ends_with("VC")) |>
    cols_label(
      starts_with("PSI") ~ md("$\\psi$"),
      starts_with("CI") ~ md("$95\\%~\\text{CI}$"),
      starts_with("Pval") ~ md("$p$")
    ) |>
    tab_options(table.font.size = "8pt", column_labels.font.size = "8pt") |>
    style_manuscript_table(table_type = "supplementary")
})

tbl_s5
```

\newpage

### Table S6: Out-of-sample validation performance {.unnumbered}

```{r}
#| label: tbl-s6
#| tbl-cap: "**Out-of-sample validation segmentation performance metrics.** Performance metrics of the segmentation of the hippocampus on the out-of-sample validation ADNI subset of data using our trained CNN. Overall-agreement and class-wise performance metrics include accuracy, Dice coefficient, Cohen's Kappa, sensitivity and specificity, with separate values reported for the left and right hemispheres and stratified by clinical group (CH, MCI, and AD). All values are reported as medians with standard deviations."

rds_path <- here("tables/table-s6_man-seg_hcv-hvr_val.rds")

tbl_s6 <- load_or_generate(rds_path, function() {
  supp_validation_data[
    order(MSR),
    .(VAL = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", median(VAL), sd(VAL))),
    .(
      GROUP = factor(GROUP, labels = sprintf("**%s**, N: 20", c("CH", "MCI", "AD"))),
      MSR = str_to_title(MSR),
      SIDE
    )
  ] |>
    dcast(... ~ SIDE, value.var = "VAL") |>
    gt(rowname_col = "MSR", groupname_col = "GROUP", process_md = TRUE) |>
    cols_align("center", columns = c("L", "R")) |>
    tab_spanner(label = "Hippocampus", columns = c("L", "R")) |>
    cols_label(ends_with("L") ~ "Left", ends_with("R") ~ "Right") |>
    style_manuscript_table(table_type = "supplementary")
})

tbl_s6
```

\newpage

### Table S7: Out-of-sample validation ANOVA {.unnumbered}

```{r}
#| label: tbl-s7
#| tbl-cap: "**Out-of-sample validation two-way mixed ANOVA results.** Results of the robust mixed two-way ANOVA with trimmed means applied to the hippocampal segmentation performance measures on the out-of-sample validation ADNI subset of data using the trained CNN, comparing clinical groups and hemispheres. Reported are the F-statistics, degrees of freedom, and *p*-values. *P*-values have been adjusted for FDR using the Benjamini and Hochberg procedure."

rds_path <- here("tables/table-s7_man-seg_anova_val.rds")

tbl_s7 <- load_or_generate(rds_path, function() {
  supp_comparisons$Validation$ANOVA[
    order(MSR),
    .(Fstat, DF, Pval = sprintf("%.3f", Padj)),
    .(
      MSR = str_to_title(MSR),
      COMP = factor(
        COMP,
        levels = c("GROUP", "SIDE", "GROUP:SIDE"),
        labels = c("**Group**", "**Side**", "**Group:Side**")
      )
    )
  ] |>
    gt(rowname_col = "MSR", groupname_col = "COMP", process_md = TRUE) |>
    tab_stub_indent(rows = everything(), indent = 1) |>
    cols_align("center", columns = c("Fstat", "DF", "Pval")) |>
    cols_label(
      "Fstat" ~ md("*F*"), "DF" ~ "df", "Pval" ~ md("*p*")
    ) |>
    style_manuscript_table(table_type = "supplementary")
})

tbl_s7
```

\newpage

## Supplementary Figures {.unnumbered}

### Figure S1: Out-of-sample validation segmentation performance {.unnumbered}

```{r}
#| label: fig-s1
#| fig-cap: "**Out-of-sample validation segmentation performance.** Violin plots of the distributions of the overlap and accuracy metrics (Dice, Kappa, and Sensitivity) between manual and computed segmentations of the hippocampus from the left (red) and right (blue) hemispheres on an out-of-sample subset of ADNI data using CNN for each of the clinical groups (CH, MCI, and AD). The median and standard deviation are represented inside the violin plots."
#| fig-width: 5
#| fig-height: 2.5

if (!is.null(supp_validation_data)) {
  supp_validation_data[
    !MSR %like% "ACC|SPEC",
    .(
      SIDE = SIDE |> factor(labels = c("Left", "Right")),
      ROI = factor("Hippocampus"),
      MSR = MSR |> str_to_title() |> factor(),
      GROUP,
      VAL
    )
  ] |>
    ggplot(mapping = aes(x = GROUP, y = VAL)) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 9) +
    theme(
      text = element_text(size = 9),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 7),
      axis.title.x = element_markdown(),
      legend.position = "none"
    ) +
    geom_violin(
      mapping = aes(fill = SIDE),
      position = position_dodge(width = 0.95),
      scale = "width",
      linewidth = 0.3,
      alpha = .02
    ) +
    stat_summary(
      mapping = aes(colour = SIDE),
      position = position_dodge(width = 0.95),
      fun.data = median_hilow,
      geom = "pointrange",
      size = 0.15,
      linewidth = 0.3
    ) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c("darkred", "midnightblue")) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("darkred", "midnightblue")) +
    facet_grid(rows = vars(ROI), cols = vars(MSR), scales = "free_y") +
    labs(
      y = "Overlap/Accuracy",
      x = paste(
        "Clinical group (<span style='color:darkred;'>Left</span> &amp;",
        "<span style='color:midnightblue;'>Right</span>)"
      )
    )
} else {
  fig_path <- here("quarto/figures/fig-s1.png")
  knitr::include_graphics(fig_path)
}
```

\newpage

### Figure S2: Out-of-sample validation volume correlations {.unnumbered}

```{r}
#| label: fig-s2
#| fig-cap: "**Out-of-sample validation: Relationship between manual and computed hippocampal volumes.** Scatter plots of the volumes (measured in cc) of the left and right hippocampus from the manual segmentations against those obtained from CNN on the out-of-sample subset of ADNI data for each of the clinical groups (CH, MCI, AD). The Spearman's rank correlation coefficients ($\\rho$) and their significance are added for each hemisphere."
#| fig-width: 4
#| fig-height: 4

if (!is.null(val_vols)) {
  val_vols |>
    ggplot(aes(x = CNN, y = MAN, colour = GROUP)) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 10) +
    theme(
      text = element_text(size = 10),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.title.x = element_markdown(),
      legend.position = "none"
    ) +
    geom_abline(
      intercept = 0, slope = 1,
      colour = cbPalette[1], linetype = "dashed"
    ) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", alpha = .2, linewidth = .5) +
    geom_point(size = 2, shape = 21) +
    ggpubr::stat_cor(
      aes(label = after_stat(r.label)),
      r.accuracy = 0.001, size = 2.7,
      cor.coef.name = "rho",
      label.x.npc = "right", label.y.npc = "bottom",
      hjust = "inward", method = "spearman"
    ) +
    facet_grid(rows = vars(ROI), cols = vars(SIDE), scales = "free") +
    scale_colour_manual(values = cbPalette[c(2, 3, 8)]) +
    labs(
      y = "Manually segmented volumes",
      x = paste(
        "Volumes segmented by CNN for:",
        sprintf("<span style='color:%s;'>CH</span>,", cbPalette[2]),
        sprintf("<span style='color:%s;'>MCI</span>, and", cbPalette[3]),
        sprintf("<span style='color:%s;'>AD</span>", cbPalette[8])
      )
    )
}
```

\newpage

### Figure S3: Out-of-sample validation Bland-Altman plots {.unnumbered}

```{r}
#| label: fig-s3
#| fig-cap: "**Out-of-sample validation Bland-Altman plots.** Bland-Altman plots for the volumetric comparison between mean HCvol (in cc) resulting from the automatic segmentation of the out-of-sample subset of ADNI data for each of the clinical groups (CH, MCI, AD). The dashed lines represent the mean difference (orange) and the upper and lower limits of agreement (blue)."
#| fig-width: 5
#| fig-height: 2.5

if (!is.null(val_vols)) {
  ba_dt <- val_vols[
    , .(
      AVG = (CNN + MAN) / 2,
      DIFF = CNN - MAN
    ),
    .(SIDE, ROI, GROUP)
  ][
    , .(
      AVG, DIFF,
      MEAN_DIFF = mean(DIFF),
      CI_low = mean(DIFF) - 1.96 * sd(DIFF),
      CI_high = mean(DIFF) + 1.96 * sd(DIFF)
    ),
    .(ROI, GROUP)
  ]

  ggplot(ba_dt, aes(x = AVG, y = DIFF)) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 10) +
    theme(
      text = element_text(size = 10),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.title.x = element_markdown(),
      legend.position = "none"
    ) +
    geom_point(shape = 21, colour = cbPalette[1]) +
    geom_hline(
      data = ba_dt[, .SD[1], .(ROI, GROUP)],
      aes(yintercept = MEAN_DIFF),
      colour = cbPalette[2], linetype = "dashed", alpha = .7
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = ba_dt[, .SD[1], GROUP],
      aes(x = 1.2, y = MEAN_DIFF, label = round(MEAN_DIFF, 2)),
      size = 2.5
    ) +
    geom_hline(
      data = ba_dt[, .SD[1], .(ROI, GROUP)],
      aes(yintercept = CI_low),
      colour = cbPalette[3], linetype = "dashed", alpha = .7
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = ba_dt[, .SD[1], GROUP],
      aes(x = 1.2, y = CI_low, label = round(CI_low, 2)),
      nudge_y = -.100, size = 2.5
    ) +
    geom_hline(
      data = ba_dt[, .SD[1], .(ROI, GROUP)],
      aes(yintercept = CI_high),
      colour = cbPalette[3], linetype = "dashed", alpha = .7
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = ba_dt[, .SD[1], GROUP],
      aes(x = 1.2, y = CI_high, label = round(CI_high, 2)),
      nudge_y = .1, size = 2.5
    ) +
    xlim(.95, 6.2) +
    facet_grid(rows = vars(ROI), cols = vars(GROUP), scales = "free") +
    labs(
      x = "Mean computed & manual volumes",
      y = "Computed - manual volumes"
    )
}
```

\newpage

### Figure S4: Permutation tests - MALF {.unnumbered}

```{r}
#| label: fig-s4
#| fig-cap: "**Permutation tests for differences in correlation strength between HCvol and HVR using MALF segmentations.** Distributions of the differences in Spearman's rank correlation coefficients ($\\Delta\\rho$ = $\\rho$(HCvol) - $\\rho$(HVR)) with respect to age, memory, and global cognition. The hypothesis tested is that the MALF-based HVR measure yields stronger correlations in magnitude than the MALF-based HCvol measure. Each distribution was obtained via 10,000 one-tailed permutation tests; shaded orange area represents the region of rejection of the null hypothesis ($\\alpha$ < 0.05). Vertical lines mark the observed $\\Delta\\rho$; solid and red when significant, black and dashed when not. FDR-corrected p-values are provided as labels."
#| fig-width: 6.5
#| fig-height: 5.7

# Check for pre-computed permutation results
perms_rds <- here("data/rds/adni-bl_hcv-hvr_corrs_non-parametric.rds")
fig_path <- here("quarto/figures/fig-s4.png")

if (file.exists(perms_rds)) {
  # Load pre-computed results and generate plot
  corr.lst <- readRDS(perms_rds)

  if ("PERMS" %in% names(corr.lst) && "CONTR" %in% names(corr.lst)) {
    library(ggridges)
    library(ggnewscale)

    mtd <- "malf"
    subDT <- corr.lst$CONTR[
      corr.lst$PERMS[mtd, on = "METHOD"],
      on = .(DX, COVAR, METHOD)
    ][
      ,
      .(Rdiff, Pval, P_diff, SIGN = Pval < 0.05),
      .(DX = factor(DX, levels = c("CH", "MCI", "AD")), COVAR)
    ]

    ggplot(subDT, aes(x = P_diff, y = DX)) +
      theme_classic(base_size = 9) +
      theme(
        text = element_text(size = 9),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.position = "none"
      ) +
      facet_grid(rows = vars(DX), cols = vars(COVAR), scales = "free_y") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = cb_palette[2:1]) +
      stat_density_ridges(
        mapping = aes(fill = factor(after_stat(quantile))),
        data = subDT[!"Memory", on = "COVAR"],
        geom = "density_ridges_gradient",
        calc_ecdf = TRUE,
        quantiles = 0.05,
        scale = 1
      ) +
      new_scale_fill() +
      scale_fill_manual(values = cb_palette[1:2]) +
      stat_density_ridges(
        mapping = aes(fill = factor(after_stat(quantile))),
        data = subset(subDT, COVAR == "Memory"),
        geom = "density_ridges_gradient",
        calc_ecdf = TRUE,
        quantiles = 0.95,
        scale = 1
      ) +
      geom_vline(
        aes(xintercept = Rdiff, colour = SIGN, linetype = SIGN),
        subDT[, .(Rdiff = unique(Rdiff), SIGN), .(DX, COVAR)]
      ) +
      scale_linetype_manual(values = c("longdash", "solid")) +
      scale_colour_manual(values = c("black", "darkred")) +
      geom_richtext(
        aes(label = sprintf("<i>p</i> = %.3f", V1)),
        subDT[, unique(Pval), .(DX, COVAR)],
        inherit.aes = FALSE,
        colour = "Black",
        fill = "White",
        alpha = .9,
        size = 3,
        x = 0,
        y = -Inf,
        vjust = -0.25
      ) +
      labs(x = expression("Difference of " * rho), y = NULL)
  } else {
    knitr::include_graphics(fig_path)
  }
} else {
  knitr::include_graphics(fig_path)
}
```

\newpage

### Figure S5: Permutation tests - NLPB {.unnumbered}

```{r}
#| label: fig-s5
#| fig-cap: "**Permutation tests for differences in correlation strength between HCvol and HVR using NLPB segmentations.** Distributions of the differences in Spearman's rank correlation coefficients ($\\Delta\\rho$ = $\\rho$(HCvol) - $\\rho$(HVR)) with respect to age, memory, and global cognition. The hypothesis tested is that the NLPB-based HVR measure yields stronger correlations in magnitude than the NLPB-based HCvol measure. Each distribution was obtained via 10,000 one-tailed permutation tests; shaded orange area represents the region of rejection of the null hypothesis ($\\alpha$ < 0.05). Vertical lines mark the observed $\\Delta\\rho$; solid and red when significant, black and dashed when not. FDR-corrected p-values are provided as labels."
#| fig-width: 6.5
#| fig-height: 5.7

perms_rds <- here("data/rds/adni-bl_hcv-hvr_corrs_non-parametric.rds")
fig_path <- here("quarto/figures/fig-s5.png")

if (file.exists(perms_rds)) {
  corr.lst <- readRDS(perms_rds)

  if ("PERMS" %in% names(corr.lst)) {
    library(ggridges)
    library(ggnewscale)

    mtd <- "nlpb"
    subDT <- corr.lst$CONTR[
      corr.lst$PERMS[mtd, on = "METHOD"],
      on = .(DX, COVAR, METHOD)
    ][
      ,
      .(Rdiff, Pval, P_diff, SIGN = Pval < 0.05),
      .(DX = factor(DX, levels = c("CH", "MCI", "AD")), COVAR)
    ]

    ggplot(subDT, aes(x = P_diff, y = DX)) +
      theme_classic(base_size = 9) +
      theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank(), strip.text = element_text(size = 10), legend.position = "none") +
      facet_grid(rows = vars(DX), cols = vars(COVAR), scales = "free_y") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = cb_palette[2:1]) +
      stat_density_ridges(aes(fill = factor(after_stat(quantile))), data = subDT[!"Memory", on = "COVAR"],
                          geom = "density_ridges_gradient", calc_ecdf = TRUE, quantiles = 0.05, scale = 1) +
      new_scale_fill() +
      scale_fill_manual(values = cb_palette[1:2]) +
      stat_density_ridges(aes(fill = factor(after_stat(quantile))), data = subset(subDT, COVAR == "Memory"),
                          geom = "density_ridges_gradient", calc_ecdf = TRUE, quantiles = 0.95, scale = 1) +
      geom_vline(aes(xintercept = Rdiff, colour = SIGN, linetype = SIGN),
                 subDT[, .(Rdiff = unique(Rdiff), SIGN), .(DX, COVAR)]) +
      scale_linetype_manual(values = c("longdash", "solid")) +
      scale_colour_manual(values = c("black", "darkred")) +
      geom_richtext(aes(label = sprintf("<i>p</i> = %.3f", V1)), subDT[, unique(Pval), .(DX, COVAR)],
                    inherit.aes = FALSE, colour = "Black", fill = "White", size = 3, x = 0, y = -Inf, vjust = -0.25) +
      labs(x = expression("Difference of " * rho), y = NULL)
  } else {
    knitr::include_graphics(fig_path)
  }
} else {
  knitr::include_graphics(fig_path)
}
```

\newpage

### Figure S6: Permutation tests - CNN {.unnumbered}

```{r}
#| label: fig-s6
#| fig-cap: "**Permutation tests for differences in correlation strength between HCvol and HVR using CNN segmentations.** Distributions of the differences in Spearman's rank correlation coefficients ($\\Delta\\rho$ = $\\rho$(HCvol) - $\\rho$(HVR)) with respect to age, memory, and global cognition. The hypothesis tested is that the CNN-based HVR measure yields stronger correlations in magnitude than the CNN-based HCvol measure. Each distribution was obtained via 10,000 one-tailed permutation tests; shaded orange area represents the region of rejection of the null hypothesis ($\\alpha$ < 0.05). Vertical lines mark the observed $\\Delta\\rho$; solid and red when significant, black and dashed when not. FDR-corrected p-values are provided as labels."
#| fig-width: 6.5
#| fig-height: 5.7

perms_rds <- here("data/rds/adni-bl_hcv-hvr_corrs_non-parametric.rds")
fig_path <- here("quarto/figures/fig-s6.png")

if (file.exists(perms_rds)) {
  corr.lst <- readRDS(perms_rds)

  if ("PERMS" %in% names(corr.lst)) {
    library(ggridges)
    library(ggnewscale)

    mtd <- "cnn"
    subDT <- corr.lst$CONTR[
      corr.lst$PERMS[mtd, on = "METHOD"],
      on = .(DX, COVAR, METHOD)
    ][
      ,
      .(Rdiff, Pval, P_diff, SIGN = Pval < 0.05),
      .(DX = factor(DX, levels = c("CH", "MCI", "AD")), COVAR)
    ]

    ggplot(subDT, aes(x = P_diff, y = DX)) +
      theme_classic(base_size = 9) +
      theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank(), strip.text = element_text(size = 10), legend.position = "none") +
      facet_grid(rows = vars(DX), cols = vars(COVAR), scales = "free_y") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = cb_palette[2:1]) +
      stat_density_ridges(aes(fill = factor(after_stat(quantile))), data = subDT[!"Memory", on = "COVAR"],
                          geom = "density_ridges_gradient", calc_ecdf = TRUE, quantiles = 0.05, scale = 1) +
      new_scale_fill() +
      scale_fill_manual(values = cb_palette[1:2]) +
      stat_density_ridges(aes(fill = factor(after_stat(quantile))), data = subset(subDT, COVAR == "Memory"),
                          geom = "density_ridges_gradient", calc_ecdf = TRUE, quantiles = 0.95, scale = 1) +
      geom_vline(aes(xintercept = Rdiff, colour = SIGN, linetype = SIGN),
                 subDT[, .(Rdiff = unique(Rdiff), SIGN), .(DX, COVAR)]) +
      scale_linetype_manual(values = c("longdash", "solid")) +
      scale_colour_manual(values = c("black", "darkred")) +
      geom_richtext(aes(label = sprintf("<i>p</i> = %.3f", V1)), subDT[, unique(Pval), .(DX, COVAR)],
                    inherit.aes = FALSE, colour = "Black", fill = "White", size = 3, x = 0, y = -Inf, vjust = -0.25) +
      labs(x = expression("Difference of " * rho), y = NULL)
  } else {
    knitr::include_graphics(fig_path)
  }
} else {
  knitr::include_graphics(fig_path)
}
```

\newpage

### Figure S7: Permutation tests - FreeSurfer {.unnumbered}

```{r}
#| label: fig-s7
#| fig-cap: "**Permutation tests for differences in correlation strength between HCvol and HVR using FreeSurfer segmentations.** Distributions of the differences in Spearman's rank correlation coefficients ($\\Delta\\rho$ = $\\rho$(HCvol) - $\\rho$(HVR)) with respect to age, memory, and global cognition. The hypothesis tested is that the FreeSurfer-based HVR measure yields stronger correlations in magnitude than the FreeSurfer-based HCvol measure. Each distribution was obtained via 10,000 one-tailed permutation tests; shaded orange area represents the region of rejection of the null hypothesis ($\\alpha$ < 0.05). Vertical lines mark the observed $\\Delta\\rho$; solid and red when significant, black and dashed when not. FDR-corrected p-values are provided as labels."
#| fig-width: 6.5
#| fig-height: 5.7

perms_rds <- here("data/rds/adni-bl_hcv-hvr_corrs_non-parametric.rds")
fig_path <- here("quarto/figures/fig-s7.png")

if (file.exists(perms_rds)) {
  corr.lst <- readRDS(perms_rds)

  if ("PERMS" %in% names(corr.lst)) {
    library(ggridges)
    library(ggnewscale)

    mtd <- "fs6"
    subDT <- corr.lst$CONTR[
      corr.lst$PERMS[mtd, on = "METHOD"],
      on = .(DX, COVAR, METHOD)
    ][
      ,
      .(Rdiff, Pval, P_diff, SIGN = Pval < 0.05),
      .(DX = factor(DX, levels = c("CH", "MCI", "AD")), COVAR)
    ]

    ggplot(subDT, aes(x = P_diff, y = DX)) +
      theme_classic(base_size = 9) +
      theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank(), strip.text = element_text(size = 10), legend.position = "none") +
      facet_grid(rows = vars(DX), cols = vars(COVAR), scales = "free_y") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = cb_palette[2:1]) +
      stat_density_ridges(aes(fill = factor(after_stat(quantile))), data = subDT[!"Memory", on = "COVAR"],
                          geom = "density_ridges_gradient", calc_ecdf = TRUE, quantiles = 0.05, scale = 1) +
      new_scale_fill() +
      scale_fill_manual(values = cb_palette[1:2]) +
      stat_density_ridges(aes(fill = factor(after_stat(quantile))), data = subset(subDT, COVAR == "Memory"),
                          geom = "density_ridges_gradient", calc_ecdf = TRUE, quantiles = 0.95, scale = 1) +
      geom_vline(aes(xintercept = Rdiff, colour = SIGN, linetype = SIGN),
                 subDT[, .(Rdiff = unique(Rdiff), SIGN), .(DX, COVAR)]) +
      scale_linetype_manual(values = c("longdash", "solid")) +
      scale_colour_manual(values = c("black", "darkred")) +
      geom_richtext(aes(label = sprintf("<i>p</i> = %.3f", V1)), subDT[, unique(Pval), .(DX, COVAR)],
                    inherit.aes = FALSE, colour = "Black", fill = "White", size = 3, x = 0, y = -Inf, vjust = -0.25) +
      labs(x = expression("Difference of " * rho), y = NULL)
  } else {
    knitr::include_graphics(fig_path)
  }
} else {
  knitr::include_graphics(fig_path)
}
```

\newpage

### Figure S8: Permutation tests - CNN vs FreeSurfer HVR {.unnumbered}

```{r}
#| label: fig-s8
#| fig-cap: "**Permutation tests for differences in correlation strength of HVR between CNN and FreeSurfer segmentations.** Distributions of the differences in Spearman's rank correlation coefficients ($\\Delta\\rho$ = $\\rho$(CNN) - $\\rho$(FS)) with respect to age, memory, and global cognition. The hypothesis tested is that the CNN-based HVR measure yields stronger correlations in magnitude than the FreeSurfer-based HVR measure. Each distribution was obtained via 10,000 one-tailed permutation tests; shaded orange area represents the region of rejection of the null hypothesis ($\\alpha$ < 0.05). Vertical lines mark the observed $\\Delta\\rho$; solid and red when significant, black and dashed when not. FDR-corrected p-values are provided as labels."
#| fig-width: 6.5
#| fig-height: 5.7

perms_rds <- here("data/rds/adni-bl_hcv-hvr_corrs_non-parametric.rds")
fig_path <- here("quarto/figures/fig-s8.png")

if (file.exists(perms_rds)) {
  corr.lst <- readRDS(perms_rds)

  if ("PERMS" %in% names(corr.lst)) {
    library(ggridges)
    library(ggnewscale)

    mtd <- "CNN-FS6"
    subDT <- corr.lst$CONTR[
      corr.lst$PERMS[mtd, on = "METHOD"],
      on = .(DX, COVAR, METHOD)
    ][
      ,
      .(Rdiff, Pval, P_diff, SIGN = Pval < 0.05),
      .(DX = factor(DX, levels = c("CH", "MCI", "AD")), COVAR)
    ]

    ggplot(subDT, aes(x = P_diff, y = DX)) +
      theme_classic(base_size = 9) +
      theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank(), strip.text = element_text(size = 10), legend.position = "none") +
      facet_grid(rows = vars(DX), cols = vars(COVAR), scales = "free_y") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = cb_palette[2:1]) +
      stat_density_ridges(aes(fill = factor(after_stat(quantile))), data = subDT[!"Memory", on = "COVAR"],
                          geom = "density_ridges_gradient", calc_ecdf = TRUE, quantiles = 0.05, scale = 1) +
      new_scale_fill() +
      scale_fill_manual(values = cb_palette[1:2]) +
      stat_density_ridges(aes(fill = factor(after_stat(quantile))), data = subset(subDT, COVAR == "Memory"),
                          geom = "density_ridges_gradient", calc_ecdf = TRUE, quantiles = 0.95, scale = 1) +
      geom_vline(aes(xintercept = Rdiff, colour = SIGN, linetype = SIGN),
                 subDT[, .(Rdiff = unique(Rdiff), SIGN), .(DX, COVAR)]) +
      scale_linetype_manual(values = c("longdash", "solid")) +
      scale_colour_manual(values = c("black", "darkred")) +
      geom_richtext(aes(label = sprintf("<i>p</i> = %.3f", V1)), subDT[, unique(Pval), .(DX, COVAR)],
                    inherit.aes = FALSE, colour = "Black", fill = "White", size = 3, x = 0, y = -Inf, vjust = -0.25) +
      labs(x = expression("Difference of " * rho), y = NULL)
  } else {
    knitr::include_graphics(fig_path)
  }
} else {
  knitr::include_graphics(fig_path)
}
```
